content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File 
Date 
: October 31, 1978 
Barbara Campbell 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Subject: 
Government Code Section 84214 
At our October 30, 1978, opinion request meeting, the 
staff concluded late contribution reports should be filed 
with the offices that received pre-election campaign 
statements and, if known at the time of the making of the 
late contributions, the offices that will receive the 
post-election campaign statement. 
If the office that will 
receive the post-election campiagn statement is not known 
at the time of the making of the late contribution, the maker 
should exercise his or her best judgment in sending the late 
contribution report. 
In imposing fines for late contribution reports, the 
staff urged that filing officers should exercise reasonable 
judgment in fining for late contribution reports. 
A mailgram is a telegram for notification purposes of 
Government Code Section 84214. 
In the joint venture arrange-
ment with the U.S. Postal Service for mailgrams, Western 
Union sends a telegram to the post office for the addressee 
(by using the name of the city and zip code) and then the 
post office in turn delivers the mailgram to the addressee 
in the next day's mail delivery.

sections.question_synthetic: Where should late contribution reports be filed under Government Code Section 84214, and what standards should apply to filing and enforcement?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Late contribution reports should be filed with offices that received pre-election campaign statements and, if known, offices that will receive post-election statements. Filers should exercise best judgment if the post-election recipient is unknown. Filing officers should exercise reasonable judgment when imposing fines, and mailgrams are acceptable notification methods.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Internal FPPC memorandum to file rather than traditional advice letter format with discrete Q/C sections. No formal question or conclusion headers present. Content appears to be staff guidance from October 30, 1978 opinion request meeting. Mailgram definition and postal service delivery mechanism included as supporting detail.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Where should late contribution reports be filed under Government Code Section 84214, and what standards should apply to filing and enforcement?

CONCLUSION: Late contribution reports should be filed with offices that received pre-election campaign statements and, if known, offices that will receive post-election statements. Filers should exercise best judgment if the post-election recipient is unknown. Filing officers should exercise reasonable judgment when imposing fines, and mailgrams are acceptable notification methods.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To File Date : October 31, 1978 Barbara Campbell From FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Subject: Government Code Section 84214 At our October 30, 1978, opinion request meeting, the staff concluded late contribution reports should be filed with the offices that received pre-election campaign statements and, if known at the time of the making of the late contributions, the offices that will receive the post-election campaign statement. If the office that will receive the post-election campiagn statement is not known at the time of the making of the late contribution, the maker should exercise his or her best judgment in sending the late contribution report. In imposing fines for late contribution reports, the staff urged that filing officers should exercise reasonable judgment in fining for late contribution reports. A mailgram is a telegram for notification purposes of Government Code Section 84214. In the joint venture arrange- ment with the U.S. Postal Service for mailgrams, Western Union sends a telegram to the post office for the addressee (by using the name of the city and zip code) and then the post office in turn delivers the mailgram to the addressee in the next day's mail delivery.
embedding.summary: FPPC staff memorandum addressing the filing requirements for late contribution reports under Government Code Section 84214, establishing guidance on where reports should be submitted and standards for enforcement.
